output:
The MoE lane (Qwen3-30B-A3B) wins the serving-lane bakeoff on a Spark because it delivers an 8.5× speed advantage over the dense 32B lane while maintaining comparable memory usage, and raw speed is the metric that ultimately determines the winning lane. This conclusion is drawn from the explicit comparison in the Hermes Serving Lane on a DGX Spark field note, which quantifies the speed gap and states that the router buys the speed for free, making speed the decisive factor.
Citations: [article_hermes_serving_lane_on_spark]

The patent‑specialist post‑mortem identified that the trainer was functioning correctly; the failure stemmed from a 56 % leakage of the synthetic pipeline’s internal state into the training signal, which was a problem with the corpus rather than the trainer.
Citations: [article_fine_tune_data_prep_decisions_on_spark]

The rerank step dominates latency, taking 523 ms per query, which is far higher than the retrieval modes themselves.
Citations: [article_rerank_fusion_retrieval_on_spark]

The sandbox tax is about 26 seconds of steady‑state overhead on top of the raw inference time, as shown in the NemoClaw sandbox results where the second‑prompt wall‑clock is 52 s versus 26 s for raw Ollama.
Citations: [article_nemoclaw_vs_openclaw_dgx_spark]
The public context retrieved here does not provide information about that. Citations: []

The Kepler model card recommends the Q4_K_M variant because it offers a good balance of size and performance, fitting within a 4 GB consumer GPU while providing decent throughput and accuracy for chat tasks. Orionfold recommends the smallest quant (Q4_K_M) when the primary goal is to fit the model on limited hardware and prioritize speed over the highest possible quality, as it delivers the highest tokens per second and sustained load minutes among the available variants.
Citations: [artifact_kepler_gguf]

Orionfold Arena is a single-screen cockpit for running, comparing, and scoring local language models on one NVIDIA DGX Spark, providing live telemetry, a leaderboard of published models, and a way to evaluate and select the best model from a personal collection without leaving the machine. It is designed for solo builders who have accumulated local models and need a tool to turn that shelf into something they can use and fly with.
Citations: [product_orionfold_arena]

The book argues that the gap is a trust gap: organizations do not trust that AI agents will behave predictably, can be audited, will contain failures, and will withstand regulatory shifts, which is why only 11% have agents running in production despite models being capable enough.
Citations: [book_ch_09]

The Orionfold Arena cockpit is built within a monorepo that includes both the BUILDER and PUBLISHER components on Spark, and it is published to orionfold.com via a separate MARKETER storefront that links to external artifact hosts such as PyPI, Hugging Face, and GitHub. The static web preview is defined in the arena-distribution.md guide, which describes the pipeline from the monorepo to the static preview and the live contract, and the storefront positioning is detailed in arena-storefront-marketing.md, which clarifies that the preview is local-runnable and not a hosted SaaS.
Citations: [doc_guides_arena_distribution_md]

Route: _GUIDES/arena-storefront-marketing.md because it explicitly governs storefront positioning and card/detail layout for orionfold.com, including the distribution pipeline and CTA structure.
Citations: [doc_guides_arena_storefront_marketing_md]

The spec that defines Arena and fieldkit as the local AI Workbench product is spec_orionfold_local_ai_workbench_v1, which describes them as bundled components of the local AI workbench for building, evaluating, governing, and operating enterprise AI artifacts on NVIDIA hardware.
Citations: [spec_orionfold_local_ai_workbench_v1]

Route: doc_fieldkit_docs_api_publish_md because it directly describes the publishing side of the Orionfold production line and the Hugging Face repository creation process.
Citations: [doc_fieldkit_docs_api_publish_md]
